content.text:
Done. There's now a `hChat.app` in `/Applications`.

**How to use it:**
- Open it from Spotlight (`⌘-Space` → "hChat"), Launchpad, or by double-clicking in Finder/Applications.
- It launches detached automatically — no terminal, no `&disown`.
- It just execs `/opt/homebrew/bin/hchat`, which is the stable brew symlink, so it keeps working after `brew upgrade hchat`. Only the version strings in `Info.plist` go stale (cosmetic — they only show in Finder's Get Info).

**First launch:** macOS Gatekeeper may say it's from an unidentified developer (the launcher isn't code-signed). If so: right-click the app → **Open** → **Open**, once. After that it opens normally.

Two notes:
- **No icon** — it'll show the generic app icon since there's no `.icns` in the repo. If you want, I can generate one and wire it into the bundle (and optionally add a `cargo-bundle`/`Makefile` target so this whole thing is reproducible per release instead of hand-built).
- This bundle is local-only and not tracked by brew. Want me to add a `make install-app` target (or a `scripts/` helper) to the repo so it's repeatable on a fresh machine?

content.text: The formula just drops the prebuilt `hchat` binary into `bin` — no app bundle. To make every Homebrew user get a launchable app, the change goes in the tap repo (`heath0xff/homebrew-tap`), and there are two real ways to do it with meaningfully different tradeoffs. Let me get your call before I touch the tap.

questions.question: Which distribution mechanism do you want for the launchable app?

options.label: Homebrew Cask (recommended)
options.description: Add a Cask to the tap. Release must ship hChat.app. Install becomes `brew install --cask heath0xff/tap/hchat`. Homebrew auto-installs to /Applications, clears Gatekeeper quarantine, shows in Spotlight. Idiomatic and robust, but changes the install command and the release artifact (CI must build the .app).

options.label: Formula builds the bundle
options.description: Keep `brew install hchat` unchanged. The formula's install step generates hChat.app from the binary and links it into /Applications. No release-pipeline change, same command, but it's non-idiomatic — `brew audit` will warn and writing to /Applications from a formula can be fragile.

options.label: Formula + caveat (manual)
options.description: Formula builds hChat.app inside the Cellar and prints a one-line command the user runs once to symlink it into /Applications. Cleanest for `brew audit`, but not automatic — each user must run the command.

file.content:
name: Release

on:
  push:
    tags:
      - "v*"

permissions:
  contents: write

env:
  FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24: true

jobs:
  build-linux:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5

      - name: Install Rust
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable

      - name: Install system deps
        run: |
          sudo apt-get update
          sudo apt-get install -y libxcb-render0-dev libxcb-shape0-dev libxcb-xfixes0-dev \
            libxkbcommon-dev libssl-dev libgtk-3-dev

      - name: Build
        run: cargo build --profile dist

      - name: Package binary
        run: |
          cd target/dist
          tar czf ../../hchat-linux-x86_64.tar.gz hchat
          cd ../..

      - name: Build .deb
        run: |
          cargo install cargo-deb
          cargo deb --no-build --profile dist

      - name: Upload artifacts
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v5
        with:
          name: linux-artifacts
          path: |
            hchat-linux-x86_64.tar.gz
            target/dist/debian/*.deb

  build-macos-arm64:
    runs-on: macos-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5

      - name: Install Rust
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable

      - name: Build
        run: cargo build --profile dist

      - name: Create .app bundle
        run: |
          cargo install cargo-bundle
          cargo bundle --profile dist

      - name: Package binary
        run: |
          cd target/dist
          tar czf ../../hchat-macos-arm64.tar.gz hchat
          cd ../..

      - name: Package .app
        run: |
          cd target/dist/bundle/osx
          zip -r ../../../../hChat.app.zip hChat.app
          cd ../../../..

      - name: Upload artifacts
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v5
        with:
          name: macos-arm64-artifacts
          path: |
            hchat-macos-arm64.tar.gz
            hChat.app.zip

  build-macos-x86_64:
    runs-on: macos-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5

      - name: Install Rust
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
        with:
          targets: x86_64-apple-darwin

      - name: Build
        run: cargo build --profile dist --target x86_64-apple-darwin

      - name: Package binary
        run: |
          cd target/x86_64-apple-darwin/dist
          tar czf ../../../hchat-macos-x86_64.tar.gz hchat
          cd ../../..

      - name: Upload artifacts
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v5
        with:
          name: macos-x86_64-artifacts
          path: hchat-macos-x86_64.tar.gz

  release:
    needs: [build-linux, build-macos-arm64, build-macos-x86_64]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5

      - name: Download all artifacts
        uses: actions/download-artifact@v5
        with:
          path: artifacts
          merge-multiple: true

      - name: Create GitHub Release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
        with:
          generate_release_notes: true
          files: |
            artifacts/hchat-linux-x86_64.tar.gz
            artifacts/*.deb
            artifacts/hchat-macos-arm64.tar.gz
            artifacts/hchat-macos-x86_64.tar.gz
            artifacts/hChat.app.zip

  update-homebrew:
    needs: [release]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Download macOS artifacts
        uses: actions/download-artifact@v5
        with:
          path: artifacts
          merge-multiple: true

      - name: Generate formula
        run: |
          VERSION="${GITHUB_REF_NAME#v}"
          SHA_ARM64=$(sha256sum artifacts/hchat-macos-arm64.tar.gz | awk '{print $1}')
          SHA_X86_64=$(sha256sum artifacts/hchat-macos-x86_64.tar.gz | awk '{print $1}')

          cat > hchat.rb <<FORMULA
          class Hchat < Formula
            desc "Lightweight desktop chat client for local LLMs"
            homepage "https://github.com/heath0xFF/hChat"
            version "${VERSION}"
            license "MIT"

            on_macos do
              if Hardware::CPU.arm?
                url "https://github.com/heath0xFF/hChat/releases/download/v#{version}/hchat-macos-arm64.tar.gz"
                sha256 "${SHA_ARM64}"
              else
                url "https://github.com/heath0xFF/hChat/releases/download/v#{version}/hchat-macos-x86_64.tar.gz"
                sha256 "${SHA_X86_64}"
              end
            end

            def install
              bin.install "hchat"
            end
          end
          FORMULA

          # Remove leading whitespace from heredoc
          sed -i 's/^          //' hchat.rb

      - name: Push formula to tap
        env:
          TAP_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          git clone REDACTED tap
          mkdir -p tap/Formula
          cp hchat.rb tap/Formula/hchat.rb
          cd tap
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
          git add Formula/hchat.rb
          git diff --cached --quiet && exit 0
          git commit -m "Update hchat to ${GITHUB_REF_NAME#v}"
          git push

file.content:
If hChat can reach the endpoint but reports "No models available", you started the server but haven't loaded (LM Studio) or pulled (Ollama) a model yet.

## Install

Download the latest release from [GitHub Releases](https://github.com/heath0xFF/hChat/releases).

### macOS (Homebrew)

```bash
brew tap heath0xFF/tap
brew install hchat
```

To update to the latest release:

```bash
brew update && brew upgrade hchat
```

### macOS (manual)

```bash
# Binary
tar xzf hchat-macos-arm64.tar.gz
mv hchat /usr/local/bin/

# Or use the .app bundle
unzip hChat.app.zip -d /Applications
```

### Debian/Ubuntu

```bash
sudo dpkg -i hchat_*.deb
```

      - name: Download all artifacts
        uses: actions/download-artifact@v5
        with:
          path: artifacts
          merge-multiple: true

      - name: Create GitHub Release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
        with:
          generate_release_notes: true
          files: |
            artifacts/hchat-linux-x86_64.tar.gz
            artifacts/*.deb
            artifacts/hchat-macos-arm64.tar.gz
            artifacts/hchat-macos-x86_64.tar.gz
            artifacts/hChat-arm64.app.zip
            artifacts/hChat-x86_64.app.zip

  update-homebrew:
    needs: [release]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Download macOS artifacts
        uses: actions/download-artifact@v5
        with:
          path: artifacts
          merge-multiple: true

      - name: Generate formula
        run: |
          VERSION="${GITHUB_REF_NAME#v}"
          SHA_ARM64=$(sha256sum artifacts/hchat-macos-arm64.tar.gz | awk '{print $1}')
          SHA_X86_64=$(sha256sum artifacts/hchat-macos-x86_64.tar.gz | awk '{print $1}')
          SHA_APP_ARM64=$(sha256sum artifacts/hChat-arm64.app.zip | awk '{print $1}')
          SHA_APP_X86_64=$(sha256sum artifacts/hChat-x86_64.app.zip | awk '{print $1}')

          cat > hchat.rb <<FORMULA
          class Hchat < Formula
            desc "Lightweight desktop chat client for local LLMs"
            homepage "https://github.com/heath0xFF/hChat"
            version "${VERSION}"
            license "MIT"

            on_macos do
              if Hardware::CPU.arm?
                url "https://github.com/heath0xFF/hChat/releases/download/v#{version}/hchat-macos-arm64.tar.gz"
                sha256 "${SHA_ARM64}"
              else
                url "https://github.com/heath0xFF/hChat/releases/download/v#{version}/hchat-macos-x86_64.tar.gz"
                sha256 "${SHA_X86_64}"
              end
            end

            def install
              bin.install "hchat"
            end
          end
          FORMULA

          # Remove leading whitespace from heredoc
          sed -i 's/^          //' hchat.rb

      - name: Push formula to tap
        env:
          TAP_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          git clone REDACTED tap
          mkdir -p tap/Formula
          cp hchat.rb tap/Formula/hchat.rb
          cd tap
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
          git add Formula/hchat.rb
          git diff --cached --quiet && exit 0
          git commit -m "Update hchat to ${GITHUB_REF_NAME#v}"
          git push

name: Release

on:
  push:
    tags:
      - "v*"

permissions:
  contents: write

env:
  FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24: true

jobs:
  build-linux:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5

      - name: Install Rust
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable

      - name: Install system deps
        run: |
          sudo apt-get update
          sudo apt-get install -y libxcb-render0-dev libxcb-shape0-dev libxcb-xfixes0-dev \
            libxkbcommon-dev libssl-dev libgtk-3-dev

      - name: Build
        run: cargo build --profile dist

      - name: Package binary
        run: |
          cd target/dist
          tar czf ../../hchat-linux-x86_64.tar.gz hchat
          cd ../..

      - name: Build .deb
        run: |
          cargo install cargo-deb
          cargo deb --no-build --profile dist

      - name: Upload artifacts
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v5
        with:
          name: linux-artifacts
          path: |
            hchat-linux-x86_64.tar.gz
            target/dist/debian/*.deb

  build-macos-arm64:
    runs-on: macos-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5

      - name: Install Rust
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable

      - name: Build
        run: cargo build --profile dist

      - name: Create .app bundle
        run: |
          cargo install cargo-bundle
          cargo bundle --profile dist

      - name: Package binary
        run: |
          cd target/dist
          tar czf ../../hchat-macos-arm64.tar.gz hchat
          cd ../..

      - name: Package .app
        run: |
          cd target/dist/bundle/osx
          zip -r ../../../../hChat-arm64.app.zip hChat.app
          cd ../../../..

      - name: Upload artifacts
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v5
        with:
          name: macos-arm64-artifacts
          path: |
            hchat-macos-arm64.tar.gz
            hChat-arm64.app.zip

  build-macos-x86_64:
    runs-on: macos-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5

      - name: Install Rust
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
        with:
          targets: x86_64-apple-darwin

      - name: Build
        run: cargo build --profile dist --target x86_64-apple-darwin

      - name: Create .app bundle
        run: |
          cargo install cargo-bundle
          cargo bundle --profile dist --target x86_64-apple-darwin

      - name: Package binary
        run: |
          cd target/x86_64-apple-darwin/dist
          tar czf ../../../hchat-macos-x86_64.tar.gz hchat
          cd ../../..

      - name: Package .app
        run: |
          cd target/x86_64-apple-darwin/dist/bundle/osx
          zip -r ../../../../../hChat-x86_64.app.zip hChat.app
          cd ../../../../..

      - name: Upload artifacts
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v5
        with:
          name: macos-x86_64-artifacts
          path: |
            hchat-macos-x86_64.tar.gz
            hChat-x86_64.app.zip

  release:
    needs: [build-linux, build-macos-arm64, build-macos-x86_64]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5

      - name: Download all artifacts
        uses: actions/download-artifact@v5
        with:
          path: artifacts
          merge-multiple: true

      - name: Create GitHub Release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
        with:
          generate_release_notes: true
          files: |
            artifacts/hchat-linux-x86_64.tar.gz
            artifacts/*.deb
            artifacts/hchat-macos-arm64.tar.gz
            artifacts/hchat-macos-x86_64.tar.gz
            artifacts/hChat-arm64.app.zip
            artifacts/hChat-x86_64.app.zip

  update-homebrew:
    needs: [release]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Download macOS artifacts
        uses: actions/download-artifact@v5
        with:
          path: artifacts
          merge-multiple: true

      - name: Generate formula
        run: |
          VERSION="${GITHUB_REF_NAME#v}"
          SHA_ARM64=$(sha256sum artifacts/hchat-macos-arm64.tar.gz | awk '{print $1}')
          SHA_X86_64=$(sha256sum artifacts/hchat-macos-x86_64.tar.gz | awk '{print $1}')
          SHA_APP_ARM64=$(sha256sum artifacts/hChat-arm64.app.zip | awk '{print $1}')
          SHA_APP_X86_64=$(sha256sum artifacts/hChat-x86_64.app.zip | awk '{print $1}')

          cat > hchat.rb <<FORMULA
          class Hchat < Formula
            desc "Lightweight desktop chat client for local LLMs"
            homepage "https://github.com/heath0xFF/hChat"
            version "${VERSION}"
            license "MIT"

            on_macos do
              if Hardware::CPU.arm?
                url "https://github.com/heath0xFF/hChat/releases/download/v#{version}/hchat-macos-arm64.tar.gz"
                sha256 "${SHA_ARM64}"
              else
                url "https://github.com/heath0xFF/hChat/releases/download/v#{version}/hchat-macos-x86_64.tar.gz"
                sha256 "${SHA_X86_64}"
              end
            end

            def install
              bin.install "hchat"
            end
          end
          FORMULA

          # Remove leading whitespace from heredoc
          sed -i 's/^          //' hchat.rb

          cat > hchat_cask.rb <<CASK
          cask "hchat" do
            arch arm: "arm64", intel: "x86_64"

            version "${VERSION}"
            sha256 arm:   "${SHA_APP_ARM64}",
                   intel: "${SHA_APP_X86_64}"

            url "https://github.com/heath0xFF/hChat/releases/download/v#{version}/hChat-#{arch}.app.zip"
            name "hChat"
            desc "Lightweight desktop chat client for local LLMs"
            homepage "https://github.com/heath0xFF/hChat"

            app "hChat.app"

            zap trash: [
              "~/.config/hchat",
              "~/Library/Application Support/hchat",
              "~/Library/Application Support/hchat.db",
            ]
          end
          CASK

          sed -i 's/^          //' hchat_cask.rb

      - name: Push formula to tap
        env:
          TAP_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          git clone REDACTED tap
          mkdir -p tap/Formula tap/Casks
          cp hchat.rb tap/Formula/hchat.rb
          cp hchat_cask.rb tap/Casks/hchat.rb
          cd tap
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
          git add Formula/hchat.rb Casks/hchat.rb
          git diff --cached --quiet && exit 0
          git commit -m "Update hchat to ${GITHUB_REF_NAME#v}"
          git push

aiTitle: Create macOS app launcher for hchat

content.text:
Found it — `src/app.rs:3240`: code blocks are rendered with `TextWrapMode::Extend`, which forces them to extend past the panel edge instead of wrapping. The message area only scrolls vertically, so long lines just get clipped (exactly what you see with the "Prompt:" block).

Switching to `Wrap` so long lines wrap within the panel while still preserving explicit newlines.

content.content: The file /Users/heath/code/hChat/src/app.rs has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

lines: // Use a selectable Label rather than a non-interactive TextEdit so
-            // users can drag-select a snippet for partial copy. Label respects
-            // newlines, so the visual layout of code is preserved.
+            // users can drag-select a snippet for partial copy. Wrap long lines
+            // at the panel width — the message area scrolls vertically only, so
+            // Extend would push content off-screen with no way to read it.
+            // Explicit newlines in `body` are still honored.

content.text:
Done. The fix has three parts:

1. **`max_tokens` 32 → 2048** (`src/app.rs:766`) — the real root cause. With only 32 tokens, Qwen spends the entire budget mid-thought and the response is *pure truncated reasoning* — that's literally the "Thinking Process: 1. **Analyze..." text you see as titles.
2. **`/no_think` prefix** on the title instruction — Qwen3's soft switch to skip chain-of-thought for this one micro-request. Harmless filler to models that don't recognize it.
3. **`markdown::strip_reasoning()`** — new helper that drops `<think>`/`<thinking>` blocks (reusing the existing segmenter) before the title is sanitized, as a safety net for any model that thinks anyway. An unterminated think block yields empty → falls back to the existing truncated-first-message title.

Tests added for all three strip cases; `cargo test` green, clippy clean (`-D warnings`), release built.

Test it against your live repro:

```bash
./target/release/hchat
```

Start a **new** chat with Qwen 3.6, send a message, wait for the reply — the title should now be a real 6-word title, not the thinking dump. Note: existing "Thinking Process:..." chats are already marked auto-titled and won't self-heal; rename those via the `…` menu (or they just stay).

If it looks right, say the word and I'll ship it as `0.9.4` through the same release flow.

fix: stop reasoning-model chain-of-thought becoming chat titles

The auto-title request capped max_tokens at 32, so a thinking model
spent the whole budget mid-thought and returned only truncated

Raise the ceiling to 2048, prefix the instruction with Qwen3's
`/no_think` soft switch, and strip any `<think>`/`<thinking>` blocks
from the response before sanitizing. Unterminated reasoning yields an
empty string and falls back to the truncated-first-message title.

EOF
